params.name: Tain Bó Cúailnge
params.description:
On Dec 14, 554 AD, a bard was compiling the anecdotes he had collected.
Tain Bó Cúailnge, also known as "The Táin", is a representative work of Irish hero stories from the 18th century's Annals of the Four Masters, and is also Ireland's greatest epic of heroism. The story tells of Queen Maeve leading the Connacht Alliance to attack Ulster and capture the stud bull Donn Cuailnge. Ireland was the first colony of Britain and the first country to break free from British colonialism. Due to the ne …
